behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: This can be caught by a pure lint rule over template text; no filesystem, subprocess, or model call is required to observe leakage patterns.
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint function directly with an in-memory prompt template that includes a known phase-instruction string and assert it fails; call it with a clean template and assert it passes.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: You must execute real prompt assembly wiring (templates + interpolation + fixtures), which typically requires real modules and fixture I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt-builder/harness path with standard fixtures, capture the final assembled prompt string, and assert no leaked phase-instruction substrings/patterns are present.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is in wiring to real skill files and config, so real filesystem/module integration is needed; no live model is strictly required for this check.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real test invocation against an actual plugin/skill directory, verify skill file content is loaded (e.g., via traced source path/content hash), and assert expectations are derived from loaded context rather than embedded constants.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily a harness configurability and file-loading concern that requires real skill-file variants and real harness wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run the harness twice with identical user prompt text: once with baseline SKILL.md and once with degraded/missing SKILL.md fixture selected via harness config; assert only context source changes and execution still proceeds.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: To prove behavior truly depends on skill content (not deterministic rule checks), you need live model behavior under changed context; this depends on model output.
behaviors.test_description: Using identical user prompt text, run live-model executions against baseline and degraded SKILL.md contexts with controlled sampling settings/retries; assert degraded context causes structural test failure where baseline passes.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering/instrumentation concern in harness code that requires real artifact writing and parser wiring, but not necessarily a live external model.
behaviors.test_description: Run harness with a controlled model-response stub that returns malformed JSON; assert raw output artifact is written to disk prior to parse failure and includes unmodified text.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: Constraint logic can be validated with in-memory sample outputs and assertion functions; no external systems are required.
behaviors.test_description: Feed assertion logic crafted payloads: one with required keywords but wrong structure, and one with correct structure; assert the first fails and the second passes based on schema/field-placement checks.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: The key claim is about real model outputs producing subtle structural regressions; this requires live LLM execution and cannot be proven by static/unit fixtures alone.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model tests and evaluate each response with both a keyword-only checker and structural assertions; demonstrate at least one case where keywords pass but structural assertions fail, and treat that as regression caught.
